channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. evacuates diplomats from Middle East. And, what to expect from N.C., Texas primaries
item.description: The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The candy heir vs. chocolate skimpflation
item.description: The grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cups creator has launched a campaign against The Hershey Company, which owns the Reese's brand. He wants them to stop skimping on ingredients.
item.content:encoded: The grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cups creator has launched a campaign against The Hershey Company, which owns the Reese's brand. He wants them to stop skimping on ingredients.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Scientists make a pocket-sized AI brain with help from monkey neurons
item.description: A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.
item.content:encoded:
A new study suggests AI systems could be a lot more efficient. Researchers were able to shrink an AI vision model to 1/1000th of its original size.

(Image credit: AerialPerspective Images)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: U.S. evacuates diplomats, shuts down some embassies as war enters fourth day
item.description: The United States evacuated diplomats across the Middle East and shut down some embassies as war with Iran intensified Tuesday while President Trump signaled the conflict could turn into extended war.
item.content:encoded:
The United States evacuated diplomats across the Middle East and shut down some embassies as war with Iran intensified Tuesday while President Trump signaled the conflict could turn into extended war.

(Image credit: Saeed Jaras)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Kristi Noem set to face senators over DHS shutdown, immigration enforcement
item.description: The focus of the hearing is likely to be on how Kristi Noem is pursuing President Trump's mass deportation efforts in his second term, after two U.S. citizens were killed by immigration officers.
item.content:encoded:
The focus of the hearing is likely to be on how Kristi Noem is pursuing President Trump's mass deportation efforts in his second term, after two U.S. citizens were killed by immigration officers.

(Image credit: Carlos A. Moreno)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: College students, professors are making their own AI rules. They don't always agree
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.content:encoded: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: North Carolina and Texas have primary elections Tuesday. Here's what you need to know
item.description: The midterm elections are officially underway and contests in Texas and North Carolina will be the first major opportunity for parties to hear from voters about what's important to them in 2026.
item.content:encoded:
The midterm elections are officially underway and contests in Texas and North Carolina will be the first major opportunity for parties to hear from voters about what's important to them in 2026.

(Image credit: Rachel Jessen/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: Trump promised the MAGA base no new wars. Then he went to war with Iran
item.description: President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Melania Trump presides at UN Security Council meeting as U.S. attacks Iran
item.description: U.S. first lady Melania Trump presided over a U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday focusing on children in conflict, as the United States has joined Israel in attacking Iran.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. first lady Melania Trump presided over a U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday focusing on children in conflict, as the United States has joined Israel in attacking Iran.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. urges Americans across Middle East to leave as war with Iran intensifies, Congress expected to vote on Trump's war powers, voters head to polls for Senate primaries in North Carolina and Texas.
item.content:encoded: U.S. urges Americans across Middle East to leave as war with Iran intensifies, Congress expected to vote on Trump's war powers, voters head to polls for Senate primaries in North Carolina and Texas.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
